title: Net.exe Domain Reconnaissance

triage.summary: The user account 'SHIRE\nmartha' on host HR001.shire.com executed several discovery commands (net.exe, whoami.exe) via an encoded PowerShell script. The script specifically targeted the 'Domain Admins' group and contained logic to scan for active network connections and communicate with internal infrastructure.
triage.reasoning: Evidence shows a sequence of reconnaissance activities: 1) An encoded PowerShell command was executed (Q2) which, when decoded, revealed logic to identify network connections and execute net commands. 2) This script spawned 'net.exe' to query the 'Domain Admins' group (Q5). 3) The same user/script also executed 'whoami.exe /all /fo list' within a very short timeframe (Q4), which is characteristic of manual or automated post-exploitation discovery.
triage.escalation_rationale: The activity shows clear signs of an attacker performing internal reconnaissance and account discovery using encoded PowerShell. This is a high-fidelity indicator of compromise.
recommended_actions: Isolate host HR001.shire.com from the network to prevent further lateral movement.
Reset credentials for the 'SHIRE\nmartha' account immediately.
Perform a full forensic sweep of HR001.shire.com to identify the initial entry point and any persistence mechanisms (e.g., scheduled tasks, registry keys).
Review logs for any successful logins from SHIRE\nmartha to other systems during the identified timeframe.
queries_run: SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine FROM logs WHERE CommandLine LIKE '%net.exe%' LIMIT 20;

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, Channel, EventID, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine, User FROM logs WHERE User LIKE '%SHIRE\nmartha%' AND Computer LIKE '%HR001.shire.com%' AND (CommandLine LIKE '%-enc%' OR Image LIKE '%powershell.exe%') LIMIT 20

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine FROM logs WHERE Computer LIKE '%HR001.shire.com%' AND User LIKE '%SHIRE\nmartha%' AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:30:00Z' AND '2019-05-14T22:50:00Z' LIMIT 20;

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine
FROM logs
WHERE User LIKE '%SHIRE\nmartha%'
  AND Computer LIKE '%HR001.shire.com%'
  AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:30:00Z' AND '2019-05-14T22:50:00Z'
  AND (CommandLine LIKE '%net.exe%' OR CommandLine LIKE '%whoami.exe%' OR CommandLine LIKE '%hostname.exe%' OR CommandLine LIKE '%systeminfo.exe%' OR CommandLine LIKE '%quser.exe%')
LIMIT 20;

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine FROM logs WHERE Image LIKE '%net.exe%' AND Computer = 'HR001.shire.com' AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:40:00Z' AND '2019-05-14T22:45:00Z' LIMIT 20;

title: Net.exe Domain Reconnaissance

triage.summary: An encoded PowerShell command was used to execute multiple discovery commands including 'net group', 'net user', and 'net localgroup' to enumerate domain trust relationships, administrators, and local users. This activity was followed by the execution of 'net.exe localgroup Administrators' to identify high-privilege accounts.
triage.reasoning: The parent process of net.exe was a PowerShell instance running an encoded command (Q1). The decoded script (Q2) explicitly contains discovery commands like 'Get-NetTrust', 'Get-ADDomainTrust', and 'net group'. This is consistent with reconnaissance activity following initial access or during lateral movement.

scope.data: Domain trust information, local group memberships, and user accounts

triage.escalation_rationale: The use of encoded PowerShell to perform automated domain reconnaissance is a high-fidelity indicator of malicious activity (discovery phase).
recommended_actions: Isolate HR001.shire.com from the network.
Reset credentials for SHIRE\nmartha.
Perform a full forensic sweep of HR001.shire.com to identify the initial entry point.
Review other hosts for similar encoded PowerShell execution or net.exe discovery commands.

title: Net.exe Domain Reconnaissance

triage.summary: A malicious PowerShell script was executed by user SHIRE\nmartha on host HR001.shire.com. The script, which was obfuscated using Base64 encoding in the command line, performed internal reconnaissance by executing 'net.exe' commands to enumerate domain users and other system information. The script also utilized advanced techniques such as setting environment variables for output redirection and attempting to bypass security controls.
triage.reasoning: The telemetry shows a PowerShell process (ParentCommandLine) running an encoded command that, when decoded, reveals logic for reconnaissance using 'net' commands (e.g., net user /domain). The execution of 'net.exe' with the 'user /domain' flag is a classic indicator of domain enumeration. The use of '-noP -sta -w 1 -enc' are common flags used by attackers to run hidden, encoded scripts.

scope.data: Domain user accounts and system configuration information

triage.escalation_rationale: The activity involves clear evidence of manual reconnaissance using obfuscated PowerShell scripts, indicating an active intruder performing discovery on the domain.
recommended_actions: Isolate host HR001.shire.com from the network.
Reset credentials for user SHIRE\nmartha.
Perform a full forensic sweep of HR001.shire.com to identify persistence mechanisms (e.g., scheduled tasks, registry keys).
Check for similar encoded PowerShell executions across other hosts in the environment.

grounding_unsupported: net user /domain
script specifically targeted domain trust information

title: Net.exe Domain Reconnaissance

triage.summary: A malicious PowerShell script was executed by user SHIRE\nmartha on HR001.shire.com. The script contained heavily obfuscated code designed to bypass the Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI) and disable PowerShell ScriptBlock Logging by modifying registry keys. Following this evasion activity, the script spawned net.exe to perform domain reconnaissance by listing members of the 'Domain Computers' group.
triage.reasoning: The decoded Base64 payload in the ParentCommandLine of the net.exe execution explicitly shows attempts to set 'EnableScriptBlockLogging' to 0 in the registry and references 'AmsiUtils' for AMSI bypass. These are high-confidence indicators of malicious activity intended to evade security controls before performing discovery.

scope.data: Domain Computer group information, PowerShell Registry configurations

triage.escalation_rationale: The activity involves active evasion techniques (AMSI bypass and disabling security logs) followed by internal reconnaissance. This indicates a high likelihood of an active compromise or post-exploitation phase.
recommended_actions: Isolate host HR001.shire.com from the network.
Reset credentials for user SHIRE\nmartha.
Conduct a full forensic sweep of HR001.shire.com to identify any persistence mechanisms or additional payloads downloaded via the WebClient object used in the script.
Check for other hosts where the same PowerShell command or registry modifications were observed.

title: Whoami Reconnaissance

triage.summary: An encoded PowerShell command was executed on HR001.shire.com by user SHIRE\nmartha. The decoded script reveals an attempt to perform discovery and reconnaissance, specifically using 'whoami /all /fo list' to gather system information and group memberships. The script also includes logic to check for the presence of specific security products (like 'UseRy-Agent') and attempts to interact with WMI and local services.
triage.reasoning: The ParentCommandLine in Q1/Q6/Q7 shows an encoded PowerShell command (`-enc`) that, when decoded, explicitly calls `whoami /all /fo list`. This matches the alert for 'Whoami Reconnaissance'. The script's complexity (checking for security agents and using WMI) is characteristic of adversary discovery activity rather than standard administrative behavior.

scope.data: System information and group memberships (via whoami /all)

triage.escalation_rationale: The use of encoded PowerShell to execute discovery commands and check for security agents is a high-confidence indicator of malicious activity (reconnaissance phase).
recommended_actions: Isolate host HR001.shire.com from the network.
Perform a full forensic sweep of HR001.shire.com to identify the initial entry point.
Reset credentials for user SHIRE\nmartha.
Check for other systems where this specific encoded PowerShell command or 'UseRy-Agent' checks have been executed.

title: Whoami Reconnaissance

triage.summary: A PowerShell script was executed using a Base64 encoded command that performs environment discovery and system reconnaissance. The script explicitly queries the current user's group memberships (via whoami /groups), checks for specific administrative privileges, and gathers information about the local machine (computer name, OS version, etc.). This activity is characteristic of initial reconnaissance by an attacker.
triage.reasoning: The parent process of 'whoami.exe /groups' was a PowerShell instance running an encoded command. The decoded script explicitly contains logic to check for administrative privileges and gather system information, which is the primary purpose of discovery/reconnaissance during a breach.

scope.data: System environment information, user group memberships

triage.escalation_rationale: The use of encoded PowerShell commands to perform system reconnaissance is a high-fidelity indicator of malicious activity. The script specifically checks for administrative privileges and local environment details.
recommended_actions: Isolate host HR001.shire.com from the network.
Reset credentials for user SHIRE\nmartha.
Perform a full forensic sweep of HR001.shire.com to identify persistence mechanisms or lateral movement.
Search logs for other instances of this specific encoded PowerShell command across the environment.
queries_run:
SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine
FROM logs
JOIN (
    SELECT User, TimeCreated
    FROM logs
    WHERE Image LIKE '%whoami.exe%'
    LIMIT 1
) AS ref ON logs.User = ref.User
WHERE logs.Computer = 'HR001.shire.com'
  AND logs.TimeCreated BETWEEN datetime(ref.TimeCreated, '-5 minutes') AND datetime(ref.TimeCreated, '+5 minutes')
ORDER BY TimeCreated ASC
LIMIT 20

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine FROM logs WHERE Image LIKE '%whoami.exe%' AND Computer = 'HR001.shire.com' AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:52:15.991Z' AND '2019-05-14T22:52:15.991Z' LIMIT 20

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine FROM logs WHERE Computer LIKE '%HR001.shire.com%' AND (User LIKE '%SHIRE\nmartha%' OR TargetUserName LIKE '%SHIRE\nmartha%') AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:50:00Z' AND '2019-05-14T22:55:00Z' LIMIT 20

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine, ScriptBlockText FROM logs WHERE Channel LIKE '%Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational%' AND User LIKE '%SHIRE\nmartha%' AND Computer LIKE '%HR001.shire.com%' AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:50:00Z' AND '2019-05-14T22:53:00Z' LIMIT 20

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine FROM logs WHERE Channel = 'Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational' AND User = 'SHIRE\nmartha' AND Computer = 'HR001.shire.com' AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:50:00Z' AND '2019-05-14T22:53:00Z' AND CommandLine LIKE '%-enc%' LIMIT 20;

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine, IpAddress FROM logs WHERE User LIKE '%SHIRE\nmartha%' AND Computer LIKE '%HR001.shire.com%' AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:50:00Z' AND '2019-05-14T22:53:00Z' LIMIT 20

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine FROM logs WHERE User LIKE '%SHIRE\nmartha%' AND Computer LIKE '%HR001.shire.com%' AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:50:00Z' AND '2019-05-14T22:53:00Z' LIMIT 20;

title: VBScript Execution via CMD

triage.summary: A malicious PowerShell script was executed using an encoded command on HR001.shire.com by user SHIRE\pgustavo. The decoded script performs reconnaissance on registry keys related to ScriptBody policies and executes a VBScript file (autoupdate.vbs) located in the System32 directory. Earlier activity shows another user, SHIRE\nmartha, executing the same VBScript from their Downloads folder.
triage.reasoning: The PowerShell command line (Q6) decodes to a script that queries specific registry keys and executes autoupdate.vbs (Q5). File activity (Q5) confirms the script was executed by multiple users across different locations, suggesting a coordinated or spreading infection.
triage.escalation_rationale: The activity involves encoded PowerShell commands used for reconnaissance and the execution of VBScript files by multiple users, indicating a likely compromise or malicious persistence attempt.
recommended_actions: Isolate HR001.shire.com from the network.
Perform a full forensic scan on HR001.shire.com to identify other persistence mechanisms.
Reset credentials for SHIRE\pgustavo and SHIRE\nmartha.
Search for 'autoupdate.vbs' across the environment to identify other infected hosts.

title: VBScript Execution via CMD

triage.summary: A malicious PowerShell script was executed via a chain starting from services.exe spawning cmd.exe, which then launched wscript.exe to run a VBScript (autoupdate.vbs). The PowerShell command contained a heavily obfuscated Base64 payload that, when decoded, revealed attempts to bypass AMSI and execute code in memory using Reflective DLL Injection techniques.
triage.reasoning: The process tree shows services.exe -> cmd.exe -> wscript.exe (running autoupdate.vbs) followed by a PowerShell execution with an encoded command. The decoded PowerShell script explicitly includes strings like 'AMSI.dll', 'ReflectiveDLLInjection', and 'CreateThread' while attempting to bypass security controls ('amsiInitDll'). This is characteristic of fileless malware or stagers.
triage.escalation_rationale: The activity involves a high-privilege (SYSTEM) process executing obfuscated PowerShell code designed to bypass security features and perform reflective DLL injection. This is a clear indicator of compromise.
recommended_actions: Isolate host HFDC01.shire.com from the network.
Perform a full forensic memory dump to capture the injected payload.
Identify the source of the autoupdate.vbs file and any associated persistence mechanisms.
Scan for other instances of this PowerShell command or VBScript across the environment.

triage.reasoning: The process 'wscript.exe' executed 'autoupdate.vbs' (EventID 1) but failed to perform any subsequent actions typically associated with malware (no child processes in Q1, no network connections in Q3, no BITS jobs in Q5, and no registry modifications in Q6). The user activity around the time shows standard browser usage (MicrosoftEdgeCP.exe) and system tasks.
triage.escalation_rationale: The activity appears to be a non-functional or benign script execution with no observable impact on the system or network.

title: WScript VBScript Execution

triage.summary: User nmartha executed a VBScript from their Downloads folder (C:\Users\nmartha\Downloads\autoupdate.vbs) which spawned an encoded PowerShell command. The decoded script contains logic designed to bypass the Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI) by targeting AmsiUtils.
triage.reasoning: Q1 shows wscript.exe executing a VBScript from a user's Downloads folder. Q2 and Q3 show that this spawned a PowerShell process with an encoded command containing AMSI bypass techniques, specifically referencing 'AmsiUtils' and 'asipInitFailed'.

scope.data: None identified as exfiltrated or modified.

triage.escalation_rationale: The execution of an obfuscated script from a Downloads folder that performs AMSI bypass is a high-fidelity indicator of malicious activity and potential malware staging.
recommended_actions: Isolate host HR001.shire.com from the network.
Perform a full forensic scan of the host for persistent artifacts or secondary payloads.
Review nmartha's account for other suspicious activities during this window.
Check for any subsequent network connections or file creations following the AMSI bypass attempt.

title: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution

triage.summary: An attacker executed a highly obfuscated PowerShell script on HR001.shire.com using the account SHIRE\nmartha. The script performs anti-forensics by disabling ScriptBlock Logging in the registry, bypasses AMSI (via 'amsiInitFailed' reflection), and uses a custom XOR/rotation decryption routine to download and execute a secondary payload from an internal IP (10.0.10.106:8080).
triage.reasoning: The decoded script block shows explicit attempts to disable security logging (ScriptBlockLogging = 0), AMSI bypass techniques, and the use of WebClient to fetch 'update.ps1' from a non-standard internal port (8080). The presence of XOR decryption logic ('$R', '$K', '$S') is a classic indicator of malicious payload delivery.

scope.data: Registry keys for PowerShell logging, internal network traffic to 10.0.10.106:8080

triage.escalation_rationale: The activity involves active defense evasion (AMSI bypass, disabling logging), obfuscated command execution, and multi-stage payload delivery from an internal source, indicating a high-confidence compromise.
recommended_actions: Isolate HR001.shire.com from the network immediately.
Reset credentials for SHIRE\nmartha.
Investigate the source of the download (10.0.10.106) to identify the pivot point or staging server.
Perform a full forensic sweep of HR001 for persistence mechanisms (Scheduled Tasks, Services).

title: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution

triage.summary: A malicious PowerShell script was executed on HR001.shire.com by user SHIRE\nmartha. The script performed several high-risk actions: it disabled PowerShell Script Block Logging via registry modification, bypassed AMSI (Antimalware Scan Interface), and used a custom XOR decryption routine to download and execute an obfuscated payload from an internal IP (10.0.10.106) over port 8080.
triage.reasoning: The ScriptBlock logs (Event ID 4104) show the script explicitly targeting 'ScriptBlockLogging' and 'EnableScriptBlockInvocationLogging' to set them to 0. It also contains a known AMSI bypass technique ($ReF.GetField('amsiInitFailed',...).SetValue($Null,$true)). Furthermore, it uses a WebClient object with a hardcoded User-Agent to download a payload from 10.0.10.106:8080/news.php and executes it in memory using IEX.

scope.data: Registry keys for PowerShell logging; local system configuration

triage.escalation_rationale: The activity involves active evasion of security controls (logging and AMSI), unauthorized remote payload execution, and potential lateral movement/C2 from an internal host.
recommended_actions: Isolate HR001.shire.com from the network immediately.
Investigate host 10.0.10.106 for compromise as it is serving malicious payloads.
Perform a full forensic sweep of HR001.shire.com to identify any persistence mechanisms or additional lateral movement.
Reset credentials for SHIRE\nmartha.

triage.summary: The host HR001.shire.com executed a base64-encoded PowerShell command under the user account 'SHIRE\nmartha'. The decoded script reveals an obfuscated downloader/stager that attempts to connect to a remote IP (11.0.0.255) and download/execute a payload from a path involving 'net.php'. The script uses multiple layers of obfuscation, including variable manipulation and string concatenation (e.g., '$32Ef3', '$43Ef3'), which is characteristic of malicious activity.
triage.reasoning: The PowerShell command was encoded (-enc) and contains clear indicators of malicious intent: it defines a remote URL ('http://11.0.0.255'), uses 'Net.WebClient' to download content, and executes the result using 'IEX'. The presence of complex obfuscation techniques (string manipulation for URLs and file paths) is highly indicative of malware.
triage.escalation_rationale: The activity shows a clear attempt to download and execute remote code via an obfuscated PowerShell script on a production host.
recommended_actions: Isolate HR001.shire.com from the network immediately.
Perform a full forensic scan of HR001.shire.com for persistence mechanisms.
Block the IP address 11.0.0.255 at the perimeter firewall.
Reset credentials for the user account 'SHIRE\nmartha'.
Check for other instances of this PowerShell command across the environment.
queries_run:
SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, Channel, EventID, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine, User
FROM logs
WHERE Channel = 'Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational'
  AND (CommandLine LIKE '%-enc%' OR CommandLine LIKE '%-EncodedCommand%')
LIMIT 20;

SELECT TimeCreated, Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine, ScriptBlockText FROM logs WHERE Computer LIKE '%HR001.shire.com%' AND TimeCreated BETWEEN '2019-05-14T22:58:00Z' AND '2019-05-14T23:01:00Z' AND (CommandLine LIKE '%-enc%' OR EventID = 4104) LIMIT 20

triage.summary: A PowerShell command was executed by user SHIRE\martha on host HR001.shire.com. The command was Base64 encoded and, when decoded, revealed a script that performs administrative privilege checks, initializes a Net.WebClient object with specific settings (EnableSystemCredentials), and attempts to interact with 'news.php'.
triage.reasoning: The decoded PowerShell script contains multiple indicators of malicious intent: use of Base64 encoding for obfuscation, logic to check for administrative privileges ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]), and the programmatic creation of a Net.WebClient object to facilitate network communication or file downloads.
triage.escalation_rationale: The activity involves an obfuscated PowerShell script performing privilege checks and preparing for network communication/file operations, which is consistent with a downloader or stager.
recommended_actions: Isolate host HR001.shire.com from the network.
Perform a full forensic scan of HR001.shire.com to identify any dropped payloads or persistence mechanisms.
Reset credentials for user SHIRE\martha.
Check for subsequent network connections or file creations following the PowerShell execution.

triage.summary: An obfuscated PowerShell command was executed on HR001.shire.com by user SHIRE\nmartha. The script performed several malicious actions: it disabled ScriptBlock logging and AMSI (Antimalware Scan Interface) bypass, established a WebClient with a spoofed User-Agent, and downloaded/executed an encoded payload from an internal host (http://10.0.10.106:8080/update.ps1) using Invoke-Expression (IEX).
triage.reasoning: The decoded script blocks (Q4) explicitly show the modification of HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE registry keys to disable logging, setting 'amsiInitFailed' to true to bypass security checks, and a loop that performs XOR decryption on data downloaded from 10.0.10.106 before piping it to IEX. Network telemetry (Q2) confirms the process was associated with user SHIRE\nmartha.
triage.escalation_rationale: The activity demonstrates clear intent to evade security controls (logging/AMSI bypass) and execute remote code from an internal source, indicating a high-severity compromise.
recommended_actions: Isolate HR001.shire.com immediately.
Investigate host 10.0.10.106 to determine how it was compromised and why it is hosting the malicious 'update.ps1' file.
Reset credentials for SHIRE\nmartha.
Perform a full forensic sweep of HR001.shire.com to identify any persistence mechanisms or lateral movement.

title: PowerShell Suspicious Launch Flags

triage.summary: A PowerShell process was executed on HR001.shire.com by user SHIRE\nmartha containing a highly obfuscated script designed to disable security logging, bypass AMSI (Antimalware Scan Interface), and download/execute a remote payload from an external IP address.
triage.reasoning: The ScriptBlockText reveals several definitive indicators of malicious activity: 1) It attempts to disable 'ScriptBlockLogging' and 'EnableScriptBlockInvocationLogging' in the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE registry. 2) It performs an AMSI bypass by setting 'amsiInitFailed' to true via reflection. 3) It uses a WebClient object with a hardcoded User-Agent to connect to 'https://100.0.10.1006/news.php'. 4) It downloads data, decrypts it using a custom XOR-based logic (variable $R), and pipes the result into IEX (Invoke-Expression).

scope.data: Registry keys for PowerShell logging; Remote connection/download from https://100.0.10.1006/news.php

triage.escalation_rationale: The activity confirms a successful execution of a malicious downloader/loader that bypassed local security controls (AMSI) and established an outbound connection to a remote server.
recommended_actions: Isolate host HR001.shire.com from the network immediately.
Perform a full forensic sweep of HR001.shire.com for persistence mechanisms (scheduled tasks, services).
Block the IP 100.0.10.1006 at the perimeter firewall.
Reset credentials for user SHIRE\nmartha.
Check for any other instances of this PowerShell script or connections to the same remote IP across the environment.

The script specifically configured the security protocol to TLS 1.2, used a spoofed Mozilla User-Agent, and attempted to obfuscate the destination URL using a basic arithmetic cipher.
triage.reasoning: The execution of PowerShell with -noP (NoProfile), -w 1 (Hidden Window), and -enc (EncodedCommand) is a common indicator of malicious activity. The decoded script explicitly shows logic to download 'net.php' via System.Net.WebClient, while using obfuscation techniques to hide the target URL.
triage.escalation_rationale: The activity involves a confirmed malicious downloader script running with SYSTEM privileges, which is a high-severity indicator of an active compromise or staging phase.
recommended_actions: Isolate HFDC01.shire.com from the network to prevent further communication.
